title: [Manectric] Battle Guide
summary: A mono-[Electric] special [pivot] that leans on [volt-switch] tempo and [lightning-rod] insurance.

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Manectric] is a clean mono-[Electric] special [pivot]. Base 105 special attack and base 105 Speed land in an awkward slice of the tier that matters — fast enough to outrun most of the middle metagame, strong enough to chip with [volt-switch] instead of committing. The base form's identity is tempo, not raw damage, and [lightning-rod] lets it slide into doubles-friendly lines where allies happily click [Electric] moves in its direction.

## Strengths
[lightning-rod] is an outstanding defensive ability — it neutralises opposing [Electric] attacks while raising special attack by one stage, which means the very [pivot] moves that normally chip [Manectric] feed it a free boost. Base 105 Speed outruns every non-scarfer in the bulky middle tier, and [volt-switch] generates free damage while also rotating in a teammate. [flamethrower] breaks the [Grass] and [Steel] answers that normally wall the [Electric] STAB, and [thunder-wave] cripples anything trying to set up on a slow [Manectric].

## Weaknesses & Answers
Mono [Electric] has zero game plan versus [Ground] types, so most plays revolve around predicting the [Ground] switch-in and volt-switching out instead of committing. The 70/60/60 bulk means [Manectric] can never survive a clean choice-specs hit, and any priority user will finish it after [focus-sash] breaks. The base form also lacks the raw damage of [manectric]-mega, so players must lean on tempo rather than trying to out-break the opposing breaker.

## Recommended Moveset
[thunderbolt] stays as the locked [Electric] STAB because it is the only move that cleanly finishes half-health targets. [volt-switch] is the defining [pivot] move — it chips while preserving initiative. [flamethrower] punishes the [Grass] and [Steel] switch-ins that try to wall the [Electric] STAB, and specifically punishes any [Ground] type that swaps in on a predicted [volt-switch]. [thunder-wave] rounds out the set by shutting down fast setup sweepers attempting to exploit [Manectric]'s middling damage output.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] guarantees [Manectric] survives one priority hit and still gets to [pivot] out. [magnet] is the alternative when the team prefers raw damage, boosting [Electric] STAB to roughly choice-specs territory without locking moves. [sitrus-berry] shines on longer [pivot] chains where incremental HP matters. [lightning-rod] is the mandatory ability, and Tera [Electric] doubles down on STAB power while Tera [Grass] flips the [Ground] weakness.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair [Manectric] with a [Flying] or [levitate] partner so [Ground] attacks that punish [volt-switch] are redirected harmlessly. Slot it behind a setup sweeper so [thunder-wave] clears the way for a free sweep, and run a [Grass] teammate that absorbs the [Water] types drawn in by the [Electric] STAB threat.

## Play Patterns
Lead with [Manectric] to force awkward switches, then [volt-switch] into the correct hard [counter]. Never stay in on a [Ground] type — [pivot] immediately. Save [thunder-wave] for opposing setup sweepers, and click [thunderbolt] only after [lightning-rod] has triggered to convert the boost into a meaningful KO.
